evals.prompt: Make the recognized-barcode highlights green in my MatrixScan Count app. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The app is on the default Icon style, so the skill recolors the recognized highlight by customizing the icon: it returns a BarcodeCountIcon (built with ScanditIconBuilder, green background + a background shape) from iconForRecognizedBarcode on a BarcodeCountViewListener (or via setIconForRecognizedBarcode). It does NOT switch to the Dot style just to change a color.

assertions.text: A green highlight is applied by customizing the icon on the default Icon style — iconForRecognizedBarcode (or setIconForRecognizedBarcode) returning a BarcodeCountIcon
The icon is built with ScanditIconBuilder using a green background color AND a background shape (ScanditIconShape.CIRCLE or SQUARE), wrapped in BarcodeCountIcon
It does NOT switch the view to the Dot style merely to change the color
The rest of the integration (context, camera, settings, mode, view creation, lifecycle) is unchanged

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count app, give each barcode a different highlight icon based on its data — a warning icon if the data starts with '99', otherwise the default check. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCountView.listener to a BarcodeCountViewListener and implements iconForRecognizedBarcode, returning a warning BarcodeCountIcon (e.g. EXCLAMATION_MARK) when trackedBarcode.barcode.data starts with '99' and BarcodeCountView.defaultRecognizedIcon() otherwise.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.listener is set to a BarcodeCountViewListener implementing iconForRecognizedBarcode(view, trackedBarcode)
The decision reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data and returns a distinct BarcodeCountIcon (built via ScanditIconBuilder) for the matching case
It returns BarcodeCountView.defaultRecognizedIcon() (or a sensible default BarcodeCountIcon, or null) for the non-matching case rather than inventing an API
The icon callback's return type is BarcodeCountIcon? (nullable) — returning a concrete BarcodeCountIcon or null both compile

evals.prompt: Switch my MatrixScan Count view to the Dot style and make the recognized dots blue. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill creates the BarcodeCountView with the BarcodeCountViewStyle.DOT newInstance overload and sets barcodeCountView.recognizedBrush to a blue Brush.

assertions.text: The view is created with BarcodeCountView.newInstance(..., BarcodeCountViewStyle.DOT)
barcodeCountView.recognizedBrush is set to a blue Brush (Brush with Android color ints and a Float width)
style is set via the newInstance overload (read-only after construction), not a post-init assignment
The rest of the integration is unchanged

evals.prompt: I scan against a manifest in my MatrixScan Count app (default Icon style). Make the not-in-list barcodes stand out with a red icon. File: IntegratedListScanningActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill customizes the not-in-list icon on the Icon style — returning a red BarcodeCountIcon from iconForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList on the BarcodeCountViewListener (or via setIconForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList).

assertions.text: The not-in-list highlight is recolored red by customizing its icon via iconForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList (or setIconForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList) returning a BarcodeCountIcon
It uses the not-in-list-specific hook (iconForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList), not the recognized one
It does not switch to the Dot style just to recolor (the app is on the Icon style)
